title: Mustardy Crispy Jamaican Pork Fried Rice
description: A flavorful and spicy Jamaican-style fried rice dish loaded with crispy pork tenderloin and a tangy mustard sauce. Serve in a traditional cazuela for a show-stopping meal.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a cazuela over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced scotch bonnet peppers and cook for an additional minute.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork tenderloin and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the cazuela and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the cazuela.
Add the cooked rice to the cazuela and stir to combine with the oil and spices.
Add the coconut milk and stir to combine.
Return the pork to the cazuela and stir to combine.
Season with salt, black pepper, and allspice.
Serve the Jamaican pork fried rice hot, garnished with a sprig of thyme and a drizzle of mustard sauce.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
